columns.extraction_plan: Extract 'NCT02799602' from the 'CLINICAL TRIAL INFORMATION' section on page 12.
The PubMed ID is not explicitly reported in the PDF. The DOI for the article is provided on page 1 (https://doi.org/10.1200/JC0.23.00041), which could be used to find the PubMed ID externally.

Extract 'ARASENS' from the title of the article on page 1: 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'.

Extract the list of authors from the top of page 1: 'Maha Hussain, MD¹; Bertrand Tombal, MD, PhD2; Fred Saad, MD³; Karim Fizazi, MD, PhD4; Cora N. Sternberg, MD5; E. David Crawford, MD; Neal Shore, MD7; Evgeny Kopyltsov, MD; Arash Rezazadeh Kalebasty, MD; Martin Bögemann, MD10; Dingwei Ye, MD11; Felipe Cruz, MD, PhD12; Hiroyoshi Suzuki, MD, PhD13; Shivani Kapur, MD14; Shankar Srinivasan, PhD15; Frank Verholen, MD16; Iris Kuss, MD17; Heikki Joensuu, MD18; and Matthew R. Smith, MD, PhD19'.
Extract '2023' from the publication information at the bottom of page 1: 'J Clin Oncol 00.© 2023 by American Society of Clinical Oncology'.
The document is a full publication, indicated by 'original reports' in the sidebar on page 1 and the extensive content (18 pages) including detailed methods, results, and discussion sections.

Extract 'Phase III' from the title of the article on page 1: '...in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'. This is further confirmed in the 'Trial Design' section on page 2: 'ARASENS was a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase III trial...'

This is a follow-up/subgroup analysis. The title on page 1 specifies 'by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups'. The 'METHODS' section on page 2 states: 'We conducted these subgroup analyses post hoc to determine if specific subgroups of patients by disease volume or risk would achieve greater benefit...'. Additionally, page 3 references the original ARASENS publication from 2022: 'From N Engl J Med, Smith MR, Hussain M, Saad F, et al, Darolutamide and Survival in Metastatic, Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer, 386(12), 1132-1142,9 Copyright© 2022...'
